investigation_findings.current_state: PinScope.tsx renders <CommandBar /> with no `onSubmit` prop. A command typed into the live HUD only calls `history.append` + `persistHistory`; it is never handed to `parseCommand` / `buildOperation` / `ClaudeBridge`.
investigation_findings.gap: SPEC §10 Flow C requires the CommandBar path to parse -> validate -> build an Operation -> copy to clipboard + append history -> toast. The CommandBar->operation-pipeline seam is unwired: `parseCommand` and `buildOperation` are referenced only by tests (`grep -rln 'parseCommand' src` returns only operation-parser.ts itself), and `ClaudeBridge` is instantiated nowhere outside its own file. AC-053 passes only because `claude-bridge.test.ts` calls the three primitives directly, so the matrix never sees that the HUD does not connect them.
investigation_findings.re_read: grep -rn 'onSubmit' src/runtime/PinScope.tsx -> only the import + JSX comment, no onSubmit passed to <CommandBar/>; grep -rln 'parseCommand' src -> operation-parser.ts only; grep -rn 'ClaudeBridge' src -> ClaudeBridge.ts:18 only

investigation_findings.current_state: EndpointSnapshotStore and SnapshotManager are instantiated nowhere in the runtime. PinScope.tsx contains no snapshot wiring; the `'snapshot'` shortcut (Shift+S) defined in useKeyboardShortcuts is not registered as a handler in PinScopeHud; TopBar.tsx renders no snapshot button.
investigation_findings.gap: SPEC §10 Flow D ('Shift+S -> walk all pins -> build Snapshot -> persist via /__pinscope/snapshot -> toast') and §8.5 ('TopBar ... snapshot button') are not wired end-to-end. The dev-server route and the EndpointSnapshotStore both exist and pass their unit tests, but no live trigger reaches them, so a user can never create a snapshot from the HUD. AC-042/AC-075 pass off `createSnapshot` called directly in `snapshot.test.tsx`.
investigation_findings.re_read: grep -rn 'EndpointSnapshotStore' src -> EndpointSnapshotStore.ts only; grep -n 'snapshot' src/runtime/PinScope.tsx -> no matches; grep -rn 'snapshot|Snapshot' src/runtime/components/TopBar.tsx -> no matches; useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:37 defines `snapshot` but PinScope.tsx:65-78 registers no `snapshot` handler

investigation_findings.current_state: PinScopeHud holds a `measuring` state toggled by the Shift+M `measure` shortcut and passes it to <Crosshair measuring=.../>, but never renders <MeasurementTool/>. MeasurementTool.tsx exists but is imported nowhere in PinScope.tsx.
investigation_findings.gap: SPEC §8.7 + §16 P4 DoD ('Measurement correct') require the two-click MeasurementTool overlay to be reachable in the live HUD. The `measuring` toggle currently only suppresses the Crosshair; the actual measurement overlay can never appear. The mechanism is half-wired: state exists, consuming component is dead.
investigation_findings.re_read: grep -rn 'MeasurementTool' src/runtime/PinScope.tsx -> no matches; grep -c 'measuring' src/runtime/PinScope.tsx -> 2 (state + Crosshair prop only)

investigation_findings.current_state: SelectionManager is instantiated nowhere outside SelectionManager.ts; no `useSelectedElement` hook exists (only useDevState/useHoveredElement/useKeyboardShortcuts/useViewportSize). PinScope.tsx wires only hover (useHoveredElement) into InfoPanel and renders no StatePanel.
investigation_findings.gap: SPEC §10 Flow B (click -> SelectionManager.select -> `data-pin-selected` moved -> URL hash `#select=e_N` -> InfoPanel locks) and §8.8 StatePanel are not wired into the §7.1 root. The §5 file structure even lists a `useSelectedElement` hook that does not exist. InfoPanel has no lock/click-outside/Esc handling (grep for lock/click/selected in InfoPanel.tsx finds none). AC-041 passes off SelectionManager unit tests, not the live click flow.
investigation_findings.re_read: grep -rn 'SelectionManager' src -> SelectionManager.ts only; ls src/runtime/hooks -> no useSelectedElement.ts; grep -rn 'StatePanel' src/runtime/PinScope.tsx -> no matches; grep -n 'lock|click|selected|Selection' src/runtime/components/InfoPanel.tsx -> no behavioral match

investigation_findings.current_state: handleSnapshotRequest in src/plugin/index.ts:65 answers `JSON.stringify({ ok: true, id })`. The R-15-06 snapshot-route test (plugin.test.ts:112-148) asserts `result.status === 200` and the on-disk file content, but never asserts the response body's `ok` flag.
investigation_findings.gap: The `{ ok: true }` success flag of the `/__pinscope/snapshot` route is un-asserted by any test (mutation M3 `true->false` survives). Coverage gap, not a behavior break: the file write is genuinely asserted, so the load-bearing DoD holds, but a regression to `ok: false` would pass the suite.
investigation_findings.re_read: Read src/plugin/index.ts:65 -> `res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, id }))`; Read tests/unit/plugin.test.ts:112-148 -> postThrough() resolves only `{ status }`; the test never JSON.parses the response body or checks `ok`

investigation_findings.current_state: handleHistoryRequest in src/plugin/index.ts:111 answers `JSON.stringify({ ok: true, count: entries.length })`. The R-15-07 history-route tests (plugin.test.ts:182-231) assert status 200 + the on-disk history.json content + entry count, but never assert the response body's `ok` flag.
investigation_findings.gap: The `{ ok: true, count }` success flag of the `/__pinscope/history` route is un-asserted (mutation M5 `true->false` survives). Coverage gap: the file write and the 1000-cap are genuinely asserted, but a regression to `ok: false` in the response body would pass the suite.
investigation_findings.re_read: Read src/plugin/index.ts:111 -> `res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, count: entries.length }))`; Read tests/unit/plugin.test.ts:182-231 -> postThrough() resolves only `{ status }`; written-body assertions read history.json from disk, never the HTTP response body

investigation_findings.current_state: Both route handlers call `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })` (index.ts:61 snapshot, :103 history). Every plugin.test.ts route test creates a fresh single-level temp root via `fs.mkdtempSync` and POSTs once, so the recursive multi-level mkdir branch is never exercised.
investigation_findings.gap: The `mkdirSync(..., { recursive: true })` recursion path is never tested (mutation M4 `true->false` survives). Coverage gap: with a single-level temp dir the `.pinscope/` parent already exists at depth 1, so `recursive:false` would still pass; only a nested-but-absent project root would expose the difference.
investigation_findings.re_read: Read src/plugin/index.ts:61,103 -> `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })`; Read tests/unit/plugin.test.ts:113,183,206 -> every root is `fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'pinscope-...'))` (one level), `.pinscope/snapshots` then writes one level below an existing dir

investigation_findings.current_state: CommandBar.tsx:44-60 `persistHistory` POSTs to `/__pinscope/history` guarded by `if (typeof fetch !== 'function') return;`. controls.test.tsx exercises focus/Tab/history via an injected HistoryManager + an `append` spy; no test stubs `fetch` or asserts the CommandBar actually fires the persistence POST.
investigation_findings.gap: The CommandBar -> `fetch` POST seam is un-asserted (mutation M2 `!==` -> `===` survives — flipping the guard would silently disable all history persistence and no test would fail). This compounds F-16-01: the only live persistence the CommandBar performs is itself untested at the network seam, so a break there is invisible to both the matrix and the suite.
investigation_findings.re_read: Read src/runtime/components/CommandBar.tsx:44-60 -> persistHistory() with `if (typeof fetch !== 'function') return;`; grep -rn 'persistHistory|fetch' tests/unit/runtime/controls.test.tsx -> no matches; controls.test.tsx CommandBar tests only spy on `history.append`, never on `fetch`
